objections.claim: R2 source_phrase in requirements_decomposition contains the wrong diff form for dev-time
objections.evidence: context-20260522-080646-D.json line 106: source_phrase says 'git diff --name-only $baseline_head_sha..HEAD (not work-tree inspection)'. The ..HEAD form compares committed HEAD to baseline — at dev/QA time, changes are UNCOMMITTED, so this diff returns empty. BA's own Technical Hints at ticket line 117 correctly identifies this: 'The correct diff command is git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working-tree vs baseline SHA), not git diff <baseline_head_sha>..HEAD (which compares committed HEAD to baseline — will be empty while changes are staged but not committed).' User requirement document also says 'git diff $baseline..HEAD' which is itself the ..HEAD form (ambiguous). M2, AC2, AC3 all correctly use the working-tree form. The R2 source_phrase mismatch means the Requirements Decomposition table has a literal drift from the AC text it backs.
objections.proposed_fix: Update R2 source_phrase in the requirements decomposition table to say 'agents/dev.md specifies that dev.files_modified must be computed from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working-tree vs baseline, pre-commit)' — matching M2 and AC2 exactly. The current source_phrase embeds the ..HEAD form which BA explicitly calls out as incorrect for dev/QA time in Technical Hints. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift: the AC literal text uses the correct form but the requirements_decomposition source_phrase row that backs it uses a contradictory form.
objections.user_requirement_verbatim: dev dispatch 时 capture baseline HEAD SHA + dirty/untracked snapshot；完成时 require dev.files_modified 来自实际 diff vs baseline
objections.drift_location: context-20260522-080646-D.json:106 and ticket-20260522-080646-D.md:108 (R2 source_phrase) — ..HEAD form vs M2/AC2/AC3 working-tree form

evidence_quality.rationale: BA directly read all four target files and confirmed the absence of baseline-capture and diff-derivation language in each. The evidence_type is correctly marked 'inferred' (not 'observed'). BA confirmed: (1) commands/dev.md lines 647-673 — verified, Step 8 dispatch template contains no baseline fields (QA confirmed by reading lines 630-680); (2) agents/dev.md lines 488-494 — verified, files_modified and files_created described as non-null lists with no diff-derivation constraint (QA confirmed); (3) agents/qa.md lines 396-430 — verified, Step 5 Regression Testing has no provenance check (QA confirmed); (4) agents/changelog-analyst.md lines 82-116 — verified, Phase 2 treats dev-report as authoritative without anomaly classification (QA confirmed). Incident corroborated: git log confirms commits 6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d all exist in history. QA independently verified that all 25 files in dev-report-d1e94e.json were already present in those prior commits (python3 cross-check: 25/25 already in prior commits, 0 not in prior commits).

scope_alignment.rationale: BA stayed strictly in scope: four markdown files only (commands/dev.md, agents/dev.md, agents/qa.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md). Won't Have explicitly excludes hooks, settings.json, new scripts, commit.sh, spec files, retroactive dev-report rewriting. No scope creep detected. The two-repo edge case note in Edge Cases is informational and does not expand scope to hooks or scripts. The user requirement verbatim says 'four parts: (1) commands/dev.md Step 8 captures..., (2) agents/dev.md mandates..., (3) agents/dev.md adds observed_preexisting[], (4) agents/qa.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md check provenance' — BA covers all four parts.

investigation_completeness.rationale: BA confirmed the root cause via direct file reads of all four targets and the user's verbatim incident description. BA identified the specific lines in each file that need modification (commands/dev.md:647-673, agents/dev.md:488-494, agents/qa.md:396-430, agents/changelog-analyst.md:82-116). QA independently reproduced the incident: git log confirms all four cited commits (6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d) predate the cycle. Python cross-check verified 25/25 files in dev-report-d1e94e.json were already in those commits. BA correctly identifies this as a spec omission (not a regression from a specific commit), which is accurate. The git_bisect_result.triggered=false is appropriate. BA self-review noted the codex consultation was blocked by tool-policy and substituted with 6 adversarial questions — all 6 applied findings are substantive and technically correct (unborn-repo edge case, dirty-baseline exclusion, diff-command-form distinction, baseline_head_sha in dev-report top-level, AC-1 verifiability, two-repo edge case).

affected_file_accuracy.rationale: All four target files exist and contain exactly what BA claims: (1) commands/dev.md is 1489 lines; lines 647-673 confirmed as Step 8 dispatch template with no baseline fields; (2) agents/dev.md is 972 lines; lines 488-494 confirmed as Output Format non-null lists description with no diff-derivation; (3) agents/qa.md is 1799 lines; lines 396-430 confirmed as Step 5 Regression Testing with no provenance check; (4) agents/changelog-analyst.md is 510 lines; lines 82-116 confirmed as Phase 2 file classification using dev-report as authoritative source. Line numbers verified within 2-3 lines of stated positions (exact match considering dynamic document state). Pre-existing guards cited in context JSON also verified: agents/dev.md line 490 contains 'Top-level non-null lists (CRITICAL)' text and agents/changelog-analyst.md line 113 contains 'Extract dev.files_modified[] and dev.files_created[] arrays' text.
files_verified: commands/dev.md — exists, 1489 lines, Step 8 confirmed at lines 646-673
agents/dev.md — exists, 972 lines, Output Format at lines 488-574 confirmed
agents/qa.md — exists, 1799 lines, Step 5 at lines 403-430 confirmed
agents/changelog-analyst.md — exists, 510 lines, Phase 2 at lines 79-116 confirmed

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.rationale: R2 source_phrase in requirements_decomposition uses 'git diff --name-only $baseline_head_sha..HEAD' (the ..HEAD committed-diff form). BA's own Technical Hints explicitly states this form is WRONG for dev-time. M2, AC2, AC3, and the development_approach all correctly use the working-tree form 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>'. The mismatch is between the requirements_decomposition source_phrase row and the AC literal text it is supposed to justify. Per QA agents/qa.md Dimension 5 contract: 'when QA finds that an AC's literal regex / command / verification recipe produces output unexpected by the AC text, but a different formulation of the same check actually verifies the AC's intent, QA MUST raise a spec_text_vs_execution_drift objection.' The R2 source_phrase drifts from the AC2 literal text by embedding the wrong diff form. The AC2 text (correct form) is what dev must implement; the R2 source_phrase (wrong form) could confuse dev about what to put in agents/dev.md.
spec_text_vs_execution_drift.drift_detail: R2 source_phrase: '..HEAD' form (wrong for dev-time) vs AC2 THEN clause: 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' (correct working-tree form). User requirement text 'git diff $baseline..HEAD' also uses ..HEAD but is ambiguous (could mean post-commit). BA Technical Hints line 117 resolves the ambiguity correctly but the requirements_decomposition table does not.

summary: BA analysis for item D (dev self-report provenance) is substantively sound. The incident is confirmed: all 25 files in dev-report-d1e94e.json were already present in git HEAD from prior commits. All four target files exist and contain exactly what BA claims. Line number references are accurate. Scope is correctly bounded to four markdown files only. The 5 ACs are literally verifiable by reading the target files (AC1 via grep in dispatch payload, AC2 via grep in Output Format, AC3 via grep in provenance check section, AC4 via grep in Phase 2 enrichment, AC5 via reading graceful-skip language). The self-review 6 findings are well-reasoned and all applied correctly. One minor objection: R2 source_phrase in the requirements_decomposition table uses the wrong diff form (..HEAD, which BA itself identifies as incorrect for dev/QA pre-commit time) while M2, AC2, AC3 all correctly specify the working-tree form. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift that could confuse dev about what to write in agents/dev.md. The fix is to update R2 source_phrase to say 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working-tree vs baseline, pre-commit)'. Overall recommendation: proceed to dev with the R2 source_phrase correction noted.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex was invoked but spent its session doing filesystem grep searches across thousands of files in the worktree without reaching a CODEX_FEEDBACK section. The 232KB output contained no structured CODEX_FEEDBACK items. This is treated as a parse failure.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self_review_substituted. QA performed adversarial self-review covering: (1) Is R2 source_phrase drift blocking or minor? Conclusion: minor — M2/AC2/AC3 use correct form so dev will implement correctly; R2 source_phrase is documentation drift, not AC drift. (2) Is investigation complete? Yes — git log confirms incident, file reads confirm current absence of baseline fields. (3) Are ACs literally verifiable? Yes — all 5 use grep or read method against target files. AC5 uses 'read' method which is less precise than grep but the graceful-skip language is straightforward to verify. (4) Scope drift from two-repo edge case? No — it is placed in Edge Cases (informational), not in Must Have requirements. (5) Is context JSON R2 source_phrase also affected? Yes — same ..HEAD form at line 106.

incident_corroborated.result: All 25 files in dev-report-d1e94e.json were already present in commits 6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d (25/25 match, 0 not in prior commits)
